steps.title: Use the paint coverage rate
steps.content: One gallon covers $70$ square yards. Painting an area of $w$ square yards once would require $\frac{w}{70}$ gallons.

steps.title: Account for two coats
steps.content:
Painting the same area twice doubles the amount of paint needed:
$$P=2\left(\frac{w}{70}\right)=\frac{2w}{70}=\frac{w}{35}$$
